Comment summary: Clarify computational capabilities, upload limits, runtime estimates, and pre-checks for intensive methods such as ConQuR.
Strategy: Address mainly in app/server and briefly in manuscript/response. Add concrete limits and early validation rather than long main-text benchmark discussion.
Action taken: Added public-server upload limits and pre-run validation; correction page reports current-session elapsed time and available average elapsed-time information; response explains limits and runtime guidance.
Status / remaining work: Implemented for limits/pre-checks/runtime display. Full archival benchmark table can be added later if desired.
Notes: Avoid overclaiming exact runtime for all possible datasets because runtime depends on dimensions, method parameters, and load.

Comment summary: Warn users when phenotype/target and batch are confounded or strongly correlated.
Strategy: Add advisory design-risk warning, not a hard blocker. Pair warning with mosaic plot.
Action taken: Upload workflow flags strong association between selected batch and target variables; manuscript/supplement describe mosaic plot and batch-target balance.
Primary location: Website/server; main case study; Supplementary Figure S1; response letter
Notes: State clearly that the warning identifies design risk; it cannot resolve confounding automatically.

Comment summary: Fix grammar: “source code is available”.
Strategy: Direct text correction.
Action taken: Corrected Availability statement grammar.

Comment summary: Use one consistent server URL.
Strategy: Standardize public URL globally.
Action taken: Use https://mbatchnet.com/ consistently.
Primary location: Main text; response letter; touched website/docs where relevant

Comment summary: Add method applicability scenarios and explanations of adjustable parameters.
Strategy: Put detailed user guidance in app; keep main text concise; supplement gives category mapping. Avoid subjective method recommendation.
Action taken: Correction table now includes method explanations, package/citation/source references, and parameter help text. Main text lists all 12 methods and groups them; supplement maps methods by practical categories.
Primary location: Website/server; main Features/Implementation; Supplementary Note 1; response letter
Notes: Use neutral wording: input assumptions, covariates, target labels, method parameters.

Comment summary: Handle NAs, abnormal values, outliers, and anomalous real-world inputs.
Strategy: Harden validation and error prompts. Do not add silent imputation/outlier removal because mBatchNet is not primary data-cleaning software.
Action taken: Upload validation blocks malformed numeric matrices, blank/non-numeric/NA/NaN/Inf values, row mismatch, missing batch/target, insufficient levels, all-zero sample rows, and public-limit violations. Warnings cover large uploads, all-zero feature columns, high sparsity, negative/transformed-looking values, and strong batch-target association.
Notes: Response explicitly explains why automatic imputation/outlier removal was not added.

Comment summary: Add Export Script functionality for reproducibility.
Strategy: Address reproducibility goal with server-centered bundle rather than standalone local script.
Action taken: Added separate Repro bundle that records session inputs, selected metadata variables, method parameters, validation metadata, run metadata, and session state; upload page can restore reproducibility_bundle.zip. GitHub source remains available for local execution.
Status / remaining work: Implemented as reproducibility bundle, not script export.
Notes: This avoids promising a local script that may not reproduce the server environment exactly.

Comment summary: If framed as a review article, revise Introduction to discuss method landscape and mechanisms; add more datasets.
Strategy: Clarify Application Note scope. Keep only concise method/resource context in main text; do not turn paper into review article.
Action taken: Revised Summary/Introduction/Conclusion to emphasize web-server integration, harmonized diagnostics, reproducible outputs, and user guidance.

Comment summary: Describe the anaerobic digestion 16S dataset clearly within the article.
Strategy: Add concise case-study details in main text and supplement.
Action taken: Added source/context, 231 OTUs, 75 samples, target phenol groups, five processing-date batches, treatment-duration covariate, target group sizes, and batch-size range.
Primary location: Main Results; Supplementary Figure S1; response letter

Comment summary: Manuscript does not propose a novel algorithm comparable to ConQuR/MMUPHin/PLSDA-batch.
Strategy: Agree and clarify contribution as software integration/Application Note rather than algorithm novelty.
Action taken: Reworded Summary/Introduction/Conclusion around established methods, web workflow, standardized benchmarking, and exportable outputs.
Notes: Avoid phrase that sounded awkward: “rather than as a new correction algorithm”; use natural Application Note framing.

Comment summary: Terminology confusion: software variables versus biology/statistics variables.
Strategy: Standardize terminology.
Action taken: Use “metadata variables”, “target”, “batch”, “covariates”, and “method parameters” consistently.
Primary location: Main text; website/server text where touched; response letter

Comment summary: Provide guidance/recommendations for choosing suitable methods/models.
Strategy: Provide neutral interpretation and compatibility guidance, not universal best-method recommendation.
Action taken: Added method explanations, parameter help, method category descriptions, and diagnostic interpretation guidance.
Primary location: Website/server; main Features/Implementation; Supplementary Note 1; response letter

Comment summary: Link metric definitions to figure/case-study interpretation.
Strategy: Strengthen Results narrative while keeping main text fluid.
Action taken: Results now ties ordination, distance tests, variance partitioning, and neighborhood metrics to residual batch separation, phenotype separation, variance attribution, and local mixing.
Primary location: Main Results; Supplementary Note 1; response letter

Comment summary: Include more numerical/statistical evidence, including corrected effect sizes and p-values.
Strategy: Report key values in Results and full compact table in supplement; avoid overcrowding main figure.
Action taken: Added Bray-Curtis ANOSIM R/p and PERMANOVA R2/p for uncorrected and displayed corrected outputs; Supplementary Table S1 contains full values.
Primary location: Main Results; Figure 1 caption; Supplementary Table S1; response letter
Notes: Figure points to values rather than adding too much text inside panel.

Comment summary: Clarify advantages over BatchServer.
Strategy: Keep main text concise; put fuller comparison in response.
Action taken: Introduction distinguishes BatchServer and MBECS briefly and states mBatchNet gap: browser-based microbiome abundance workflow with recent microbiome-specific methods and harmonized diagnostics.
Notes: Avoid standalone competitor table in main/supplement.

Comment summary: Add output summary so users can locate files such as corrected data.
Strategy: Implement as output bundle manifest/summary rather than main-text-heavy explanation.
Action taken: Download outputs bundle includes output_summary.json, runtime_summary.json, parameter_manifest.json, validation_report.json, figures, tables, logs, and corrected outputs.

Comment summary: Explain why the website supports more methods than those discussed/evaluated; provide comparative results for other methods.
Strategy: Main text must list all supported methods; case-study figure remains compact. Supplement maps methods. Response explains display choice.
Action taken: Summary says 12 supported methods; Features lists all 12: ConQuR, MMUPHin, PLSDA-batch, DEBIAS-M, MetaDICT, ComBat, limma, ComBat-seq, FAbatch, RUV-III-NB, FSQN, BMC. Added missing references. Supplement maps categories.
Primary location: Main Summary/Features; reference.bib; Supplementary Note 1; response letter
Status / remaining work: Implemented for full method coverage. Comparative figure remains focused for page/readability.
Notes: User explicitly requested all methods in main text; now included.

Comment summary: Define CLR before use.
Strategy: Direct definition in assessment workflow.
Action taken: Defined centered log-ratio (CLR) and Aitchison distance in main text.

Comment summary: Make abbreviation full names available for readers.
Strategy: Expand supplement and concise main diagnostic grouping.
Action taken: Supplementary Note 1 explains PCA, PCoA, NMDS, ANOSIM, PERMANOVA, pRDA, PVCA, alignment, entropy of batch mixing, and silhouette.

Comment summary: Correct MBECS capitalization.
Strategy: Direct text correction.
Action taken: Changed to MBECS.

Comment summary: Summary may imply novel batch-correction method; distinguish software integration from method innovation.
Strategy: Clarify mBatchNet integrates established methods in web workflow.
Action taken: Summary now says mBatchNet integrates established methods and 12 supported correction methods; Introduction/Conclusion emphasize workflow/diagnostics/export.

Comment summary: Clarify applicability to shotgun metagenomic datasets.
Strategy: Conservative scope statement: compatible processed feature tables only; no raw-read processing; no unvalidated shotgun benchmark claim.
Action taken: Response clarifies mBatchNet accepts compatible taxonomic/functional abundance/count tables derived after upstream profiling; upload guidance/validation states processed feature-table boundary.
Notes: Do not claim full shotgun biological validation unless a shotgun benchmark is added.

Comment summary: Explain sparsity, compositionality, zero inflation and why they complicate correction.
Strategy: Add concise Introduction explanation.
Action taken: Added sentence explaining uneven feature detection and compositional scaling effects.

Comment summary: Clarify “microbiome-oriented methods”.
Strategy: Make phrase specific.
Action taken: Changed to “microbiome-oriented batch-effect correction methods”.

Comment summary: Clarify why input requirements/parameterization/output differences create practical challenge.
Strategy: Describe assumptions and workflow heterogeneity, not just file format.
Action taken: Main text now explains methods differ in count-like vs transformed inputs, covariate support, and target-label usage.

Comment summary: Clarify distinction from MBECS.
Strategy: Concise main positioning + fuller response explanation.
Action taken: Introduction distinguishes MBECS as R-package functionality and states mBatchNet browser-based microbiome workflow gap.

Comment summary: Provide scalability/performance/runtime/memory constraints.
Strategy: Add practical server limits and runtime display; avoid exact universal runtime/memory claims without final benchmark.
Action taken: Upload limits and pre-checks are implemented; correction page reports current-session elapsed time and available average elapsed-time information.
Status / remaining work: Partially implemented for practical constraints/runtime display; final benchmark table optional/pending.
Notes: If final submission needs more, add measured runtime/memory benchmark under stated hardware.

Comment summary: Explain Aitchison distance and why appropriate for compositional microbiome data.
Strategy: Brief workflow definition in main; expanded supplement.
Action taken: Main text defines Aitchison as Euclidean distance between CLR-transformed profiles; supplement adds diagnostic context.

Comment summary: Clarify Bray-Curtis analyses operate on relative abundances.
Strategy: Direct workflow definition.
Action taken: Main text states Bray-Curtis dissimilarity is computed on relative-abundance profiles.

Comment summary: Explain how diagnostic methods contribute to evaluation.
Strategy: Group diagnostics by purpose and interpretation.
Action taken: Main text groups diagnostics into ordination, distance-matrix tests/heatmaps, variance partitioning, and neighborhood/embedding metrics; supplement expands interpretation.
Primary location: Main Features/Assessment; Supplementary Note 1; response letter
